# NATS Exporter
| Status        |           |

The NATS exporter publishes OpenTelemetry traces, metrics and logs to a
[NATS](https://nats.io) server, using either core NATS publish/subscribe or
JetStream. Each signal is serialized with a configurable encoding and published
to a configurable subject.

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Description |
| ------- | ------- | ----------- |
| `urls` (required) | | List of NATS server addresses. Provide more than one for a cluster, e.g. `["nats://localhost:4222"]`. |
| `name` | `""` | Optional connection name reported to the server. |
| `connection.timeout` | `2s` | Dial timeout when establishing a connection. |
| `connection.max_reconnects` | `60` | Maximum reconnect attempts (`-1` = infinite). Reconnection is handled by the NATS client. |
| `connection.reconnect_wait` | `2s` | Delay between reconnect attempts. |
| `tls` | (disabled) | TLS client settings ([`configtls.ClientConfig`](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/config/configtls/README.md)): `insecure`, `ca_file`, `cert_file`, `key_file`, etc. When omitted, the connection is not TLS-wrapped. |
| `auth.token.token` | | Bearer-token authentication. |
| `auth.user_password.username` / `auth.user_password.password` | | Username/password authentication. |
| `auth.nkey.seed_file` | | Path to an NKey seed file. |
| `auth.credentials.file` | | Path to a NATS credentials (`.creds`) file. |
| `jetstream` | (disabled) | When present, publish via JetStream instead of core NATS. The bound stream must already exist. |
| `jetstream.publish_timeout` | `5s` | Per-publish timeout for JetStream acknowledgements. |
| `traces.subject` | `otel.traces` | Subject traces are published to. |
| `metrics.subject` | `otel.metrics` | Subject metrics are published to. |
| `logs.subject` | `otel.logs` | Subject logs are published to. |
| `<signal>.encoding` | `otlp_proto` | Payload encoding: `otlp_proto`, `otlp_json`, or the component ID of a registered [encoding extension](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/extension/extensionencoding/README.md). |

Only **one** `auth` mechanism may be configured at a time.

This exporter also supports the standard `exporterhelper` settings:
[`timeout`](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/exporter/exporterhelper/README.md),
[`sending_queue`](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/exporter/exporterhelper/README.md),
and [`retry_on_failure`](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/config/configretry/README.md).

### Example

A minimal pipeline receiving OTLP and exporting to NATS:

```yaml
receivers:
  otlp:
    protocols:
      grpc:
        endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317

exporters:
  nats:
    urls:
      - nats://localhost:4222
    traces:
      subject: otel.traces
      encoding: otlp_proto
    metrics:
      subject: otel.metrics
    logs:
      subject: otel.logs
    auth:
      token:
        token: ${env:NATS_TOKEN}
    sending_queue:
      enabled: true
    retry_on_failure:
      enabled: true

service:
  pipelines:
    traces:
      receivers: [otlp]
      exporters: [nats]
    metrics:
      receivers: [otlp]
      exporters: [nats]
    logs:
      receivers: [otlp]
      exporters: [nats]
```

To publish through JetStream instead of core NATS, add a `jetstream` block
(the stream that binds the subject must be created out-of-band beforehand):

```yaml
exporters:
  nats:
    urls:
      - nats://localhost:4222
    jetstream:
      publish_timeout: 5s
```

## JetStream vs. core NATS

| | Core NATS | JetStream |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Delivery | Fire-and-forget, lowest latency | Persisted and acknowledged (at-least-once) |
| If no consumer is listening | Message is dropped | Message is retained in the stream |
| Server-side setup | None | A stream bound to the subject must exist first |
| Publish cost | A network round-trip (flush) | A round-trip plus a persistence ack |

Use **core NATS** when telemetry is best-effort and lowest latency matters, and
consumers are reliably attached. Use **JetStream** when you need durability and
at-least-once delivery — for example, buffering telemetry across consumer
restarts — and are willing to provision streams. With core NATS this exporter
flushes after each publish, so the `sending_queue`/`retry_on_failure` settings
see a delivery failure if the server is unreachable.
